provisions: Non-exclusive license to use System at specified location under Brand
Term: 10-15 years from Opening Date (Conversion). Set in Addendum.
No renewal right. Re-licensing at franchisor's sole discretion.
HITS and HSM agreements run concurrently with FA — auto-terminate with FA
Monthly Royalty Fee: 5.5% of GRR (Spark brand code PE)
Monthly Program Fee: 3.5% of GRR standard; 3.0% if in compliance with FA including quality/service Standards
Program Fee subject to change; max increase 1% of GRR over Term
Electronic funds transfer required for all payments
Service charges: 1.5%/month on overdue amounts
Franchisor may offset any amount owed against credits, including affiliate amounts
Must obtain prior consent for architect, designer, GC, major subs (Section 6.1.1)
Plans and Designs must be submitted per schedule in Addendum/PIP (Section 6.1.2)
No Hotel Work without written consent on Plans and Designs (Section 6.1.3)
Architect must certify ADA compliance before approval (Section 6.1.4)
No plan changes without prior written consent (Section 6.1.5)
Franchisor's approval is not a warranty of adequacy
GM and key personnel must complete required training programs (Section 4.1, 5.1.5)
Must operate 24/7 except as permitted for special circumstances
Must follow all Standards in Manual; Standards may change at any time
Must participate in quality assurance program including inspections and Stay Score
Management must be approved — either franchisee or approved Management Company
If Management Company becomes Competitor, 90 days to replace
Curable: payment default (10 days), Hotel Work delay (10 days), insurance (10 days), other defaults (30 days)

Non-curable (immediate): 16 grounds including repeat breach in 24 months, 3 notices in 12 months, bankruptcy, cease operations 5+ days, fraud, becoming Competitor, public health threat, unauthorized transfer, gaming violations
Interim remedies on default notice: suspension from Reservation Service, remove from directories, divert reservations, disable software, revoke financial accommodations
Pre-opening: $10,000 x rooms (if operator uses site for another hotel within 1 year)
Post-opening before 2nd anniversary: greater of (Avg Monthly Royalty x 60) or ($25,000 x rooms) [Note: FA uses $25K, FDD Item 6 says $10K — the FA prevails]
After 2nd anniversary before last 60 months: Avg Monthly Royalty x 60
Last 60 months: Avg Monthly Royalty x remaining months
FA characterizes this as 'agreed fee' not 'penalty or liquidated damages'
Actual damages available in lieu if: 7+ Brand FAs terminate within 12 months due to franchisee breach; or unauthorized transfer to Competitor or hotel converts to Competing Brand within 2 years
All transfers prohibited except Permitted Transfers and Change of Ownership Transfers
Permitted Transfers (no change of control): publicly traded equity, private <50%, institutional fund transfers — some without notice/consent
Permitted Transfers requiring consent: affiliate, family/trust, death, private >50% changed — $5,500 fee
Change of Ownership: new FA on then-current terms, PIP, $75,000 fee
No transfer to Competitor or Sanctioned Person
Death: to immediate family within 1 year, must meet requirements
During term: cannot own/be franchisor/licensor of competing brand in economy segment (as determined by STR)
May be franchisee of Competitor or manage Competitor properties
Post-term: No noncompete covenant
Required guarantors determined by franchisor after review of financial information and ownership
May include spouse of owner
May require third-party guaranty for lender comfort letters or as condition of consent to certain loans
May require additional guaranty upon notice of default
Must reimburse franchisor for all payments due to any claim arising from hotel operations
Must defend franchisor, Hilton Worldwide, and all affiliates, officers, directors, employees
Includes attorneys' fees and court costs
Indemnification survives termination
Governing law: New York (without NY choice-of-law principles)
Venue: US District Court ED Virginia (Alexandria) or Fairfax County VA state court; if no federal SMJ, New York NY
Franchisor may bring action where hotel is located
No arbitration except Puerto Rico (AAA or JAMS)
General release required on renewal/transfer (Section 16.8): releases all claims 'without limitation' relating to FA or relationship through Effective Date
Must maintain minimum levels and types per Manual
Must use insurers with minimum ratings specified
Must name additional insureds as specified
If franchisee fails, franchisor may purchase and bill franchisee
All information provided by franchisor is franchisor's property
All information obtained from hotel/guests/prospective guests is franchisor's property
All revenues from the Information are franchisor's property
Franchisor may use information for any purpose including FPRs in other FDDs
Franchisee may use customer data acquired from third parties during/after term for hotel operations only
Casualty: may choose to repair/rebuild if hotel reopens within 18 months
If elect not to rebuild: FA terminates, no liquidated damages if no competitor hotel at site for 3 years
Failure to operate 5+ consecutive days (not due to Force Majeure) = non-curable default
Competitor definition tied to STR market segment classification (economy for Spark)
No dedicated meeting rooms permitted (Lipscomb coexistence agreement restriction)
Mandatory Guest Fee requires franchisor consent
Strategic Partnerships are 'Other Businesses' — no franchisee protection
Economic conditions not a defense to breach
Representations and warranties: franchisee independently investigated risks, not relying on promises outside FA/FDD
